rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban in any curriculum or training surface.
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Over-hedge detector.
Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
Watch out for planting jargon too early.
Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: M4 file; bare M-references allowed per 2026-05-09 second pass (curriculum-wide convention at M2+). Huryn's three-block frame earned via quote-from-own-work per design.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
Golden path only in the body.
Student moves stay in conversation with the agent.
Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
Section headers use command verbs when the section is about doing something.
Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Memory disambiguation handled at lines 53-59 (student-authored .claude/memory/ vs Claude-Code auto-memory at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
Reflection beats stay invisible in the flow.
Body "read it" / "look at it" / "notice X" after chat-print is dead weight.
Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
